definition.name: von_neumann_algebra
definition.definition: A von Neumann algebra M is a *-subalgebra of B(H) (bounded operators on a Hilbert space) that is closed in the weak operator topology (equivalently, in the strong operator topology). By von Neumann's bicommutant theorem, M is a von Neumann algebra if and only if M = M'', where M' = {T ∈ B(H) : TS = ST ∀S ∈ M} is the commutant. A finite-dimensional von Neumann algebra is isomorphic to a direct sum ⊕_{j=1}^k M_{n_j}(C) of full matrix algebras (by Artin-Wedderburn). The normal states of M are those states that are continuous in the weak operator topology, equivalently representable as ω(T) = Tr(ρT) for a density operator ρ when M acts on a separable Hilbert space. Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §II.
